key_insight: AutoResearch uses an LLM as an offline research agent to iterate on spacecraft control training, while an embedded credibility layer only accepts gains that beat measured seed noise and verification checks.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a reproducible way to automate policy development for aerospace guidance, navigation, and control without letting the model directly control the vehicle.
method: LLM reads a plain-language problem description plus run history and proposes one script edit per iteration.
Each candidate is evaluated with measured per-problem seed noise, reseeded verification of the best configuration, and leave-one-out pruning of the agent’s edits.
The same auditable loop is applied unchanged to rendezvous and keep-out-zone docking control problems calibrated to known optimal benchmarks.
evidence_signal: Across both aerospace tasks, audited policies exceed the measured seed noise by many standard deviations, and on docking the learned policy remains feasible on every seed while undirected search finds no feasible policy.
watch_item: Results depend on the quality of the seed-noise calibration and verification pipeline, so incorrect noise estimates or evaluation leakage could weaken the auditability claim.
paper_title: Agentic AutoResearch forSpace Autonomy: An Auditable, LLM-Driven Research Agent for Aerospace Control Problems
